chunks.heading: Books Related to Sword World RPG
chunks.notes: Key claims: The Sword World RPG rulebook references several related publications including Replay Collections, Scenario Collections, World Guide, Novels, and other rulebooks. The Replay Collections are based on an older version of the rules and may have contradictions, while the Duda RPG Replay Collection has added humorous mystery elements. The Scenario Collections can be used nearly as-is but some follow the old version of rules. The World Guide introduces the setting of Alecrast. Novels and short story collections help understand the setting, though some series like Duda and Dawn of Chaos have different settings or variants. Collection Series help with game terminology. Old rulebooks exist but are superseded by the current edition. The Game Master Screen is based on the old rules and needs modifications. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-books-related-to-sword-world-rpg

chunks.heading: 13.1 Types of Monsters
chunks.notes: Enriched the 'Types of Monsters' section from Sword World RPG - Complete Edition (pages 170-172). Wrote a comprehensive summary of the 12 monster types: fae, youma, spirits, titans, mythical beasts, magical beasts, animals, plants, undead, magical creatures, daemons, humans, and others. Key claims covered include the 12 monster types based on Laverna's Natural History, spirits' life involvement and immunity to poisons/mental attacks, and the various characteristics and origins of each monster type.

chunks.heading: 13.2.4 Spirits
chunks.notes: Key claims: Will-O-Wisp is a spirit of light, appearing as a blue-white ball of light about 50 cm in diameter, and is among the most unstable spirits with little intellect. Shade is a spirit of darkness, controlling fear, and cancels out natural light within 5 meters, leaving the area in total darkness. Brownie appears in old mansions, has illusions and telekinesis, and cannot leave the mansion. Undine is a water spirit that can enter a target's lungs and cause drowning. Salamander is a spirit of fire that breathes fire and is immune to fire attacks. Sprite is a spirit that controls shame and can make others invisible. Dryad is a plant spirit that can charm and bind allies. Mad spirit of life appears as a shining white horse and impacts lives around it. Entities touched: Will-O-Wisp, Shade, Brownie, Undine, Salamander, Sprite, Dryad, Mad spirit of life. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-4-spirits.

chunks.heading: 13.2.5 Titans
chunks.notes: Written the main chapter page for 13.2.5 Titans. Updated the source summary for the entire Sword World RPG - Complete Edition. Key claims covered: mad spirit of life's age-shifting effects, ogres (jikininki) as carnivorous titans, and yeti's weakness to fire. Entities touched: mad spirit of life, valkyrie, yeti, ogre, spriggan, troll, hecatoncheir, giants (hill, forest, fire, frost), cyclops. Pages written: 1.

chunks.heading: 13.2.6 Mythical Beasts and Magical Beasts (part 1)
chunks.notes: Key claims: Satyrs are forest-dwellers with human upper bodies, goat lower bodies, and horns, using flutes to manipulate emotions. Ceiling hangers are magical beasts with asymmetrical arms and glue-like lower body, attacking sleeping prey with surprise and strangling them. Harpy creatures have human female heads and bird bodies, with deela and furia races having different traits and abilities. Hippocampi are sea creatures resembling horses with fish tails, moving quickly underwater but suffering penalties on land. Medusas are magical beasts in human female form with snakes for hair, capable of petrifying with gaze and poisonous snake attacks. Androscorpions are mythical beasts with human upper body and scorpion lower body, using spirit magic and possessing hallucinogenic poison. Entities touched: Satyr, Ceiling Hanger, Harpy, Hippocampus, Medusa, Androscorpio. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-6-mythical-beasts-and-magical-beasts-part-1.

chunks.heading: 13.2.6 Mythical Beasts and Magical Beasts (part 3)
chunks.notes: Key claims: Humbaba spits fire once every 3 rounds; fire causes a disease called humbaba curse. Roc is a giant bird, second only to dragon, with 20-meter wingspan. Lesser dragons breathe fire in 20-meter radius, elder dragons use ancient magic and roar magic. Entities touched: humbaba, roc, lesser dragon, elder dragon. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-6-mythical-beasts-and-magical-beasts-part-3.

chunks.heading: 17.2 Lost Spells
chunks.notes: Key claims: Lost spells are not taught in the guild, sorcerers must meet spell level requirements to use them, and they can be learned from sorcerers or spellbooks. Entities touched: Sorcerers, Spellbooks, Game master. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-17-2-lost-spells.

chunks.heading: 18.3 Rules for Destroying Structures
chunks.notes: Key claims: The rules for destroying structures in Sword World RPG define defense and destruction points for various materials. Wooden walls with 20 cm thickness have maximum defense points of 7 and destruction points of 15. Damage calculation determines whether a structure is destroyed, has its defense points reduced, or is unaffected. Special rules apply for fire-type magic and spirit walls. Entities touched: defense points, destruction points, spirit walls. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-18-3-rules-for-destroying-structures.

chunks.heading: Guide to Forcelia & Alecrast (part 1)
chunks.notes: Key claims: Forcelia is a flat, square world with four sealing gates; the age of mythology began with the death of a Titan who created the world and gods; the Age of Magic saw the rise and fall of the Kingdom of Kastuul; the current Age of Sword began after the fall of the Ancient Kingdom. Entities touched: The Great One (Titan), Pharis, Rahda, Marfa, Myrii, Cha Za, Phalaris, Kingdom of Kastuul, Queen June V, Pirate King Geers. Pages written: 1 (sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-guide-to-forcelia-alecrast-part-1).
